entry.title: Taipei Times: 23 Jun, 2022

entry.title: Washington Post: 22 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Russia intensifies push to seize Luhansk
  • Letters written, tanks in position as battle for Lysychansk looms
  • Maps of Russia’s invasion of Ukraine
  • Pew poll: Global views on NATO and U.S. rise; for Russia, a decline
  • The scandal over Boris Johnson job-hunting for his wife won’t go away
  • Va. couple sailing to Portugal go missing after boat damaged in storm
  • In photos and videos: the aftermath of the deadly Afghanistan earthquake
  • Russia’s chokehold over gas could send Europe back to coal
  • E.U. poised to back Ukraine’s candidate status. Here’s what it means.
  • Russia makes ‘marginal gains’ near Severodonetsk
  • Afghanistan quake kills more than 1,000, injures 1,600, officials say
  • Two Jesuit priests in Mexico are slain in area torn by drug trade
  • Turkey’s Erdogan hosts Saudi crown prince, ending rift over Khashoggi murder
  • Microsoft launches effort to slow Russian propaganda on war, vaccines
  • Ukrainian photojournalist ‘executed in cold blood’ by Russians, group says
  • Under pressure from Beijing, Hong Kong’s schools become more patriotic
  • Tributes pour in for El Chango, the Mexican narco pet monkey shot dead

  • Australian state bans Nazi swastika amid resurgence of far-right extremism
  • U.S. attorney general visits Kyiv as heavy fighting continues in Luhansk

  • Chile meets controversy in remaking the role of first lady
  • South African leader gets final chapter of corruption report
  • Former education minister in Brazil is jailed in graft probe
  • Report: Puerto Rico Senate made 'extravagant' purchases
  • Pups and people await furry finale at Westminster Dog Show

  • Fear over recent shootings has some avoiding crowds, businesses
  • Andrew Gillum, former Fla. governor candidate, charged with wire fraud
  • Asian carp threaten the Great Lakes. Will calling them ‘copi’ help?
  • Officer faces probe for telling Black driver, ‘This is how you guys get killed’
  • When a call to the police for help turns deadly
  • South Dakota AG convicted on impeachment charges, removed from office
  • Minn. city to pay $3.25M in police killing of Daunte Wright, lawyers say
  • A Tesla was in a junkyard for three weeks. Then it burst into flames.
  • Theater backs off plan to fast-forward through same-sex ‘Lightyear’ kiss
  • In rare medical procedure, these two women share one liver
  • Cousins receive mystery postcards sent decades ago: ‘I was stunned’
  • Post Politics Now: With new evidence coming in, Jan. 6 panel to continue hearings in July
  • W.Va. politician, abortion protester sentenced to 3 months for Jan. 6 role
  • Judge delays Proud Boys trial in fallout from House Jan. 6 hearings
  • Sen. Cramer says he may need part of finger amputated
  • DOJ wants to know if Sidney Powell is funding Oath Keepers’ defense
  • Alone in Washington, Rusty Bowers tells world what happened in Arizona
  • Trump’s pressure drew violence, threats to local officials, committee shows
  • Stock recovery falters after Fed chief acknowledges recession risk
  • What a pause in the gas tax would mean for prices at the pump
  • Female scientists don’t get the credit they deserve. A study proves it.
  • Formula shortage adds to financial crunch for farmworker families
  • This inflation-proof bond is paying 9.62%. Here’s how to buy it.
  • The unintended consequences of the $178 billion bailout to keep hospitals and doctors afloat
  • Top Senate Democrat casts doubt on prospect of major data privacy bill
  • Computer chip subsidies face a make-or-break moment in Congress
  • Facebook Oversight Board says company should be much more transparent
  • Landover Hills is ‘a welcoming community’
  • Who is responsible for repairing damaged insulation in a condominium?
  • Housing market slowdown takes hold as mortgage rates dig in
  • Oil Is in Another Bear Market - and for Good Reason
  • The Rich-Poor Divide on Clean Power Is Getting Wider
  • Biden’s Gas Tax Holiday Won’t Save Summer
  • Now Even Central Bankers Are Blaming Themselves
  • These Are the Gun Measures Advancing in US Congress
  • Why Fracking Won’t Solve the Global Oil and Gas Squeeze
  • A ‘Price Cap’ on Russian Oil — What Would That Mean?
  • What ‘Friend-Shoring’ Means for Trade in a Less-Friendly World
  • How many Rusty Bowers Republicans are out there?
  • ‘Stakeholder’ capitalism is parasitic progressivism
  • The U.S. door swings open to Ukrainians
  • We need laws to protect election workers like ‘Lady Ruby’ Freeman
  • The voices the Jan. 6 committee needs to hear
  • Macron lost his parliamentary majority. That shouldn’t be surprising.
  • A gas puzzler: If Biden ran Big Oil, what would he do differently?
  • If ‘Lightyear’ can spark a backlash, then no LGBTQ victories are safe
  • Restricting U.S. oil exports would betray European allies and benefit Russia
  • These Republicans defended democracy. How many will be left after 2024?
  • D.C. voters sent mixed messages in the Democratic primary
  • A question for Garland: Do you know how it feels for POTUS to target you?
  • Buried in the gun deal is a significant advance
  • When even Caterpillar flees Illinois, you know the state is in trouble

  • Why we need to modernize our cargo ships
  • The National Mall is not Disney World
  • Racism is a health crisis
  • The Browns pay the price for playing the fool
  • Don’t miss the big picture on environmental impact
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: The Sydney Morning Herald: 22 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: The Sydney Morning Herald: 22 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-22 23:07PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Wasn’t Bitcoin supposed to be a hedge against inflation?
  • Australia news LIVE: Housing shortages could cost nation $25 billion a year; Crown Sydney wins conditional licence for Barangaroo casino; energy market suspension to be lifted

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 23 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • 80% of cannabis seized at Haneda and Narita comes in liquid form
  • YamatoQ group building ‘Eden’ communities in the countryside
  • Tokyo Olympic committee lauds its own efforts to host Games
  • Public urged to remove masks outdoors to avoid heatstroke
  • L.A.-style food truck serves up tacos, builds cultural bridges
  • Fukuoka passes bylaw to protect politicians, staff from harassment
  • Hiroshima and Nagasaki mayors speak at 1st nuke ban treaty forum
  • Campaigning gets under way for July 10 Upper House election
  • Son of LDP bigwig bows out of politics, won’t run in July poll
  • Fear of possible use of nuclear arms heard at TPNW meeting
  • Utilities to offer points programs for those who conserve power
  • Sumitomo Mitsui to strengthen its collaboration with SBI Group
  • South China floods force tens of thousands to evacuate
  • South Korea launches first satellite with homegrown rocket
  • North Korean leader convenes military meeting amid tensions
  • South Korea reports first suspected monkeypox cases, tests underway
  • Taiwan scrambles jets to warn away Chinese planes in its air defense zone
  • Tailor remakes kimono left by the deceased into family keepsakes
  • TASTE OF LIFE/ Teriyaki hamburger steak made of mixed ground meat: Trio of meats created harmony that impressed his picky father
  • Urban hotels turn to rail buffs, aviation fans and dog lovers
  • Early bird hiking tour with breakfast offers best of Hiroshima
  • EDITORIAL: A high voter turnout is an absolute must in July 22 election
  • VOX POPULI: Half-baked look at government’s COVID response led to bad report
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: Guardian: 22 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war: Finland ready to fight if attacked, says general; drone causes explosion at Russian oil refinery – live
  • Sri Lanka’s prime minister says economy has ‘completely collapsed’
  • Afghanistan earthquake: at least 1,000 people killed after 5.9 magnitude tremor
  • Exposed Boehly can make Tuchel as powerful as Guardiola and Klopp | Jacob Steinberg
  • Serena Williams and Ons Jabeur sail into Eastbourne semis with new nickname
  • Ecuador facing food and fuel shortages as country rocked by violent protests
  • British man facing death sentence in Donetsk told: ‘Time is running out’
  • Los Angeles may ban new gas stations to help combat climate emergency
  • Rupert Murdoch and Jerry Hall set to divorce – report
  • Rory McIlroy brands LIV Golf players ‘duplicitous’ as Koepka is latest to move
  • Macron says parties must cooperate after he loses control of parliament
  • Eritrea women’s team footballers still in hiding after escaping in Uganda
  • Rugby’s card-happy approach to concussion is just not working | Michael Aylwin
  • Bristol couple jailed for enslaving 29 Slovakian people
  • Mites that mate on our faces at night face extinction threat
  • ‘I’d vote for him again’: Bowers backs Trump despite denouncing ‘big lie’
  • Minions: The Rise of Gru review – feeble origin story hopefully lays franchise to rest
  • Bulgaria’s pro-western government collapses after just six months
  • Bronwyn Kuss: the 10 funniest things I have ever seen (on the internet)
  • After the freeze: can Australia and China rekindle relations?
  • ‘I had to be taken out of the museum’: the artistic challenge that almost broke Dean Stevenson
  • Test captains left to field thorny questions while the suits hide at Headingley
  • Saudi crown prince visits Turkey as relations thaw after Khashoggi murder
  • Fed chief vows to keep raising rates until ‘compelling evidence’ of falling inflation
  • Europe told to get ready now for Russia to turn off all gas exports to region
  • Trevor Noah on Uvalde cops: ‘What were they waiting for? The invincible star from Mario?’
  • The secret to finding true joy online? Embracing your most cringeworthy self
  • Without a proper plan, stagflation will be the least of Britain’s problems | Larry Elliott
  • Daunte Wright’s family to receive $3.2m in settlement over police shooting
  • ‘I popped the question. Of course she said yes – she was as wasted as I was’: the Glastonbury festival I’ll never forget
  • The female orgasm: lots of throbbing and quivering – but no moaning
  • Wings of Desire review – Wim Wenders’ elegiac hymn to a broken cold-war Berlin
  • The girl power trio of Von King Park: Amy Touchette’s best photograph
  • Lose the Sheen: can you identify the film stars from their real names? – quiz
  • US woman left traumatised after Malta hospital refuses life-saving abortion
  • Baltimore Ravens’ Jaylon Ferguson dies at 26: ‘He was a wonderful young man’
  • Here’s the result of the trans swimming ban: strife, with a big role for sports lawyers | Robin Moira White
  • One of earliest known mosques found in Israeli desert, say experts
  • Drone crashes into Russian oil refinery in possible attack
  • Trump campaign knew ‘fake electors’ scheme was fraudulent, panel argues
  • Rwanda is a brutal, repressive regime. Holding the Commonwealth summit there is a sham | Michela Wrong
  • Microsoft limits access to facial recognition tool in AI ethics overhaul
  • National incident declared over polio virus findings in London sewage
  • EU plan to halve use of pesticides in ‘milestone’ legislation to restore ecosystems
  • Music for general societal exhaustion: why Ed Sheeran can't lose | Michael Cragg
  • Chinese city stripped of ‘civilised’ title after attack on female diners
  • George Michael: Freedom Uncut review – pop hero lets it all hang out
  • Brexit: unilateral action on NI protocol ‘not conducive’ to trade deal, warns US
  • Scientists unveil bionic robo-fish to remove microplastics from seas
  • Bodies of couple lay undiscovered in Irish home for 18 months
  • The January 6 hearings are damning. But Republicans don’t care | Robert Reich
  • Boxing legend Christy Martin: ‘My husband told me for 20 years he would kill me’
  • Top 10 books about terrible jobs
  • ‘Free public transport works’: a Q&A with Boston mayor Michelle Wu
  • ‘Honour killings? It should be called the devil’s work’: Bekhal Mahmod on the murder of her sister
  • The ‘clearances paradox’: could less policing actually reduce gun violence in New York?
  • A moment that changed me: a car crash altered my mother for ever – and I was sent back to boarding school
  • I took the train to Düsseldorf – here’s my guide to the city
  • More flowers, fewer cars: the rewilders turning parking spaces into parks
  • A liberating high comes with group nudity at Dark Mofo – it can turn anyone into a giggling fool | Sian Cain
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 22 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 22 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-22 22:36PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Incoming Hong Kong leader John Lee promises more aggressive approach to countering ‘fearmongering and badmouthing’ by city’s critics
  • Incoming leader John Lee working on strategy to reopen Hong Kong border with mainland China, may reduce quarantine for arrivals
  • False Covid positives leave more than 130 people stranded overnight at Hong Kong-Zhuhai-Macau Bridge
  • Senior Beijing official with over 30 years’ experience in Hong Kong affairs appointed to deputy role in nation’s top advisory body
  • Microsoft supports Hong Kong start-ups in partnership with Science Park, Cyberport
  • Hong Kong Palace Museum can help foster sense of national identity among residents, outgoing leader says
  • Owner of Hong Kong’s iconic Jumbo Floating Restaurant could claim compensation if vessel had been insured, analysts say
  • ‘Glocalised’ school-based curriculum at Caritas Wu Cheng-chung Secondary School empowers innovation and inspires students from nearly 20 countries and regions
  • Hong Kong police arrest 7 for allegedly conspiring with 3 retail outlets to cash in HK$220,000 worth of e-vouchers
  • Hong Kong to include Korean in DSE university entrance exam from 2025 amid K-Culture boom
  • Hong Kong child abuse scandal: first of 27 employees charged sentenced to 4 months’ jail
  • Hong Kong’s incoming deputy justice secretary to quit pro-Beijing group amid concerns membership could undermine impartiality
  • Veteran Hong Kong lawmaker Regina Ip appointed convenor of incoming leader John Lee’s de facto cabinet
  • CLP Power to investigate ‘why backup system failed’ to prevent Hong Kong’s worst outage in decades
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong tour group heads to Japan for the first time in more than 2 years after Covid rules eased; city logs 1,447 cases
  • What happened to Hong Kong’s Jumbo Floating Restaurant? Everything you need to know about the ill-fated tourist attraction
  • Hong Kong’s housing and economic development must be linked to the environment to create a liveable city
  • Unpacking Hong Kong’s laws, 25 years after handover: 5 rulings on Basic Law where Beijing, not the city’s courts, had final say in key cases
  • G7 pledges to counter ‘state-driven censorship’ and help protect journalists
  • Long Covid: children can have lingering symptoms but the chances are low, study finds
  • To keep up with China, Pentagon must cut red tape and embrace latest technology, top US official says
  • 6 ways China thinks it can offset zero-Covid impact to get its economy back on track
  • ‘It can create life’: Chinese team claim stem cell breakthrough in mice study
  • As China-Horn of Africa Peace Conference ends, Beijing’s envoy offers to play regional security role
  • Joe Biden’s trade chief Katherine Tai calls China tariffs ‘significant’ leverage
  • Chinese rights advocate Xu Zhiyong on trial for state subversion amid secrecy and tight security
  • US-China relations: Beijing has own ‘trade weapons’ to counter Xinjiang ‘forced labour’ law
  • Uneasy times for Hongkongers making their home in mainland China
  • At BRICS forum, China’s Xi Jinping takes aim at West over Russia sanctions
  • Taiwan navy helicopter makes hard landing, injuring four crew members
  • Bitcoin market meltdown prompts fresh warning in China that value of world’s leading cryptocurrency could fall to zero
  • China’s cash-strapped local governments accused of misusing funds as economic pressure grows
  • Tech war: Shenzhen recruits Japanese semiconductor heavyweight as China moves to challenge US, Korean dominance in memory chips
  • China’s Tangshan city stripped of ‘civilised’ status after brutal attack on women diners
  • China’s electricity demand soars as heatwaves bake northern, central provinces
  • China inflation: Li Keqiang says ‘prudent’ economic policy, bumper wheat harvest leaves room to tackle risks
  • US business group calls for ‘urgent’ talks on Taiwan free-trade deal
  • When will China’s Fujian aircraft carrier be ready for active duty?
  • How the US can help end Afghanistan’s humanitarian crisis
  • Japan raps China, Russia warships’ ‘show of force’ during Asean meeting
  • Indonesia deports Japanese Covid fraud suspect – US$7.3 million in government funds stolen
  • Cash-strapped Pakistan scores IMF financial aid win – but millions of people may face ruin, analysts say
  • Malaysia’s Ismail Sabri Yaakob unveils more handouts for low-income families as Najib, Anwar take aim at rising prices
  • Singapore’s Covid-19 infections rise amid new subvariants but no change to rules – although health ministry urges boosters
  • Myanmar minister joins Asean defence meeting as junta shifts Suu Kyi trial to prison
  • US$3 billion tax bill for Philippines’ Marcos family will be collected vows new chief – 30 years after dictator’s death
  • US bans use of anti-personnel landmines everywhere – except the Korean peninsula
  • Reef shark rescued from Singapore oil refinery
  • Filipino lawyer shot dead in US was ‘a case of mistaken identity’, official says
  • Why Asia wants more nuclear weapons – thanks to Russia’s war in Ukraine
  • Indonesian artists’ ‘anti-Semitic’ work removed from German show after upsetting Israel
  • Japan’s Kishida says talks with China key to regional peace amid rising maritime tensions
  • US Capitol riot hearings to take break as new evidence floods in
  • Russian troops ‘executed’ photographer in Ukraine, press group says
  • Russia’s Sergey Lavrov in Iran to discuss nuclear deal, cooperation
  • Rupert Murdoch and Jerry Hall are getting a divorce, US media report says
  • Saudi crown prince visits Türkiye for first time since Jamal Khashoggi murder
  • Coronavirus: French face Covid-19 upsurge, other Europeans see increase
  • Moscow accuses Germany of ‘Russophobic hysteria’ on date of Hitler’s invasion – while pounding Ukraine
  • Afghanistan earthquake kills 1,000 – toll likely to rise
  • First delivery of German heavy weaponry arrives in Ukraine as Zelensky urges West for more, quickly
  • Fate of ancient Afghan Buddhist city in hands of Taliban, Chinese copper mine consortium
  • Most significant US gun legislation in decades clears hurdle in Senate
  • Inside Ukraine’s bombarded Luhansk region: ‘Everything is on fire’
  • As China’s zero-Covid policy takes an economic toll, Hainan free-trade port could provide a boost
  • Shanghai Stock Exchange is world’s No 1 IPO venue as Chinese firms return home to counter US delisting threat
  • ESG is not a scam, but much work remains to define and standardise it
  • Hong Kong start-up EcoBricks, backed by Sino Group, upcycles ‘problem plastic’ to reduce landfill use and carbon emissions
  • Bitcoin resumes slide amid mounting concerns about recession
  • Alibaba Health, JD Health pull Chinese healthcare stocks lower amid fears of a crackdown on online pharma platforms
  • CLP Power and HK Electric adapt to help Hong Kong realise 2050 carbon-neutral goal
  • UK developer backed by Hong Kong tycoon Henry Cheng eyes US$171 million in tokenisation plan for London residential tower
  • Hong Kong stock rebound on pause after recouping US$570 billion in market value since March, as health stocks lead decline
  • Private equity fund backed by the family of Hang Seng Bank’s co-founder bets big on China’s SMEs amid fundraising slump
  • Broker exodus: high rents and electronic trading have seen more than half of Hong Kong’s brokerages leave Central since 1997
  • Swire Properties pays HK$1.96 billion for 13,200 sq ft plot in Wan Chai, beating 20 rivals in fierce competition
  • Chinese consumers exhibit more caution and price sensitivity as zero-Covid strategy fans jobs and income uncertainty: survey
  • Singapore’s CapitaLand offers ‘buy now, pay later’ plan for Vietnamese residential project to entice Hong Kong investors
  • Microsoft scraps some AI facial-analysis tools, citing risk of bias
  • Apple M2 MacBook Pro 2022 13-inch review: new chip that’s super efficient takes laptop to the next level, even if it may look the same
  • Look Up movie review: bland and patronising stories of grown-up children chasing their dreams hardly make for a celebration of 25 years of Chinese rule in Hong Kong
  • The best seafood, tempura and casual French fare in Hong Kong – a wine expert’s picks
  • No 3-Michelin-star restaurants for Dubai as the food guide gives its first awards to the city
  • Scandinavian design from living room to bedrooms and kitchen turns Hong Kong family apartment into a future-proofed home
  • 5 Hong Kong influencers reveal vulnerable sides – and their tips to overcome tough times and achieve holistic wellness
  • What is skin purging and how is it different from a breakout? Experts explain how to understand what’s happening to your face
  • How volunteering benefits your mental health and self-esteem as well as others: Hong Kong volunteers’ stories
  • How reading The Art of Retail Buying taught co-founder of sustainable fashion label Allegory everything she needed to know about business
  • British royals, undercover: Queen Elizabeth, Prince Harry, Meghan Markle, Prince William and Kate Middleton have all gone incognito – and Princess Diana even crashed a gay bar with Freddie Mercury
  • Who is Lady Sarah Chatto, Queen Elizabeth’s beloved niece? The low-key British royal is the daughter of Princess Margaret, and she attended the Platinum Jubilee with Meghan Markle and Prince Harry
  • Who is Elon Musk’s teen transgender daughter, Vivian Jenna Wilson? The 18-year-old twin is changing her name and gender – and does not ‘wish to be related to’ her billionaire father
  • Milan Men’s Fashion Week: Fendi presented denim, fringe and rubber sliders for its spring/summer 2023 collection, with an earthy colour palette in abundance
  • Milan Men’s Fashion Week: Versace invoked pop Baroque style with a sustainable pull for its spring/summer 2023 collection, with Donatella Versace at the helm
  • Who is Blackpink’s richest member? Net worths, ranked – Jennie, Lisa, Rosé and Jisoo work with Celine, Prada, Bulgari, Dior and Mac, raking in millions from Instagram and their music too
  • How much does Shah Rukh Khan charge to appear at events? The Bollywood legend performed at Isha Ambani’s wedding, but it could cost you a cool US$1 million to have him attend yours
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 22 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
